methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $10M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Nuclear Energy (found 1 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 8 same-sector peers exist in Nuclear Energy, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Fuse Energy
private.rationale: Same sector (Nuclear Energy), same stage (Series A), post-money $200M+

data.narrative:
Nuclearn.ai is a Series A-stage nuclear energy company that has raised $10.5M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a sector universe of 11 comparable companies: 3 public (OKLO, NNE, SMR) and 8 private-stage peers including Fuse Energy, TerraPower, X-Energy, Kairos Power, and Oklo.

Stage-progression methodology is the applicable triangulation approach here. Using total capital raised ($10.5M) as a valuation proxy and applying a 2.0x multiplier typical of Series A-to-next-stage progression yields an implied valuation of $21M. Peer-median analysis could not be reliably applied due to insufficient same-stage and adjacent-stage comparables within the nuclear energy sector—while 8 private peers exist, the narrow filters (same stage, adjacent stage, similar tier) did not yield the minimum 3 datapoints required for statistical confidence.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $18M to $24M, with a median point of $21M. This estimate rests primarily on stage-based step-up dynamics and should be regarded as preliminary, given reliance on a single method. Key uncertainties include technology differentiation relative to the peer set, capital efficiency, and near-term milestones that would warrant next-stage funding. Refinement would benefit from either expanded peer-stage alignment or disclosure of company-specific unit economics or development progress.
